About Ceres
Ceres is a leading developer of clean energy technology: fuel cells for power generation and electrolysers to produce green hydrogen. Its asset-light, licensing model has seen it establish partnerships with some of the world's largest companies, such as Doosan, Delta, Denso, Shell, Weichai and Thermax. Ceres' solid oxide technology supports greater electrification of our energy systems, including AI data centres, commercial and industrial applications, and produces green hydrogen at high efficiencies as a route to decarbonise emissions-intensive industries such as ammonia, steelmaking and electrofuels. Ceres is listed on the London Stock Exchange ("LSE") (LSE: CWR) and is classified by the LSE Green Economy Mark, which recognises listed companies that derive more than 50% of their activity from the green economy.
Read more on our website
www.ceres.tech
or follow us on
LinkedIn
.

RNS Number : 1381G
Ceres Power Holdings plc
29 May 2026
CWR.L
29 May 2026
Ceres Power Holdings plc
("Ceres Power" or "the Company")
Total Voting Rights, admission of further securities to trading and Block Listing application
Horsham, UK:
Ceres Power Holdings plc (CWR.L), a leading developer of clean energy technology,
confirms that as at 29 May 2026, the Company had
195,782,718
Ordinary Shares of 10 pence each ("Ordinary Shares") in issue, with one voting right each.
The Company does not hold any shares in treasury. The number of Ordinary Shares with voting rights is therefore
195,782,718
.
Accordingly, these figures may be used by shareholders as a denominator for the calculations by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or change to their interest in, the Company, under the Financial Conduct Authority's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.
In compliance with the Financial Conduct Authority's PRM Admission to Trading on a Regulated Market rules, the Company confirms that 746,992 Ordinary Shares allotted pursuant to the Company's share plans were admitted to trading over the calendar month to date. Information required in compliance with PRM 1.6.5 is detailed below:
Issuer name:
Ceres Power Holdings plc
Legal Entity Identifier (LEI):
213800N7AAHSXQUOA733

Ordinary Shares

746,992

195,782,718
Confirmation that the further transferable securities admitted to trading are fungible with transferable securities already admitted to trading:
Confirmed.
Date range of securities admitted covered by the notification
1 May 2026 to 29 May 2026.
Block Listing application
The Company has made an application to the London Stock Exchange Plc for a block listing of 242,424 Ordinary Shares. The Ordinary Shares will be allotted from time to time in accordance with the exercise of options under the Company's Long Term Incentive Plan (LTIP) and Sharesave Plan.
It is expected that the block admission will become effective on 01 June 2026.
The Ordinary Shares, when issued, shall rank equally with the Company's existing issued Ordinary Shares.

latest.title: Directorate change
latest.content:
14 May 2026 15:31
RNS Number : 4050E
Ceres Power Holdings plc
14 May 2026
CWR.L
14 May 2026
Ceres Power Holdings plc
("Ceres Power" or "the Company")
Directorate change
Horsham, UK:
In accordance with LR 6.4.6R (2), as previously reported Ceres Power announces that Trine Borum Bojsen completed her term as a Non-executive Director of the Company at the close of the AGM held 14 May 2026.
As stated in the Annual Report and Accounts of the Company for the year ended 31 December 2025, Warren Finegold, Ceres Power Holdings plc Group Chair, said:
"In view of her increased operating responsibilities at Equinor, where she has been promoted to Senior Vice President PWR Global Offshore Wind, Trine decided not to stand for re-election at the AGM. We congratulate Trine on her promotion and thank her for her many important contributions to Ceres over the last four years."

company_30s.what_they_do: CWR Ltd. makes and sells specialised electrical components for industrial automation and renewable‑energy systems, mainly high‑frequency transformers and power modules.
company_30s.why_they_matter: These parts are needed in every new battery pack, solar inverter and electric‑vehicle charging station. As the world shifts to clean power, demand for efficient power conversion keeps growing, giving CWR a foothold in a high‑growth niche.
company_30s.current_state: The firm is still small, trading in the low‑hundred‑pence range, and appears to be working through a restructuring phase. It has recently changed directors and is exploring a partnership with Delta Electronics and Centrica, hinting at a push to expand product reach. The stock sits well below its historical highs, suggesting a steep recovery could be possible if the company stabilises.

rns_narrative: The company is reshuffling its board and has signed a strategic partnership with Delta Electronics and Centrica to broaden its distribution channels in the UK and EU.
Several announcements focus on governance changes and share‑holding updates, signalling a move to improve transparency and attract institutional interest.
No new product launches or earnings surprises have been disclosed, so the focus remains on governance and partnership rather than immediate revenue drivers.

crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Some traders point to the new partnership as a potential catalyst, but overall optimism is muted because no earnings or product news has surfaced.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Others warn that the company’s thin operating history and lack of recent financial performance make it a risky play, especially if the partnership fails to materialise.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The tone has stayed flat; a few posts hint at a possible upside if the partnership delivers, but sentiment has not swelled or cooled significantly.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends shows no recent spikes in search interest, so the market is largely unaware of CWR’s developments.

near_term_catalysts: A formal announcement from Delta Electronics or Centrica about a joint product launch or distribution agreement could trigger buying interest.
Any update on the company’s financials—such as a quarterly earnings release or a funding round—would provide a clear data point for traders.
A surge in trading volume that breaks the current low‑volume plateau would signal renewed market attention.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a spike in share price above the 20‑day moving average coupled with a volume increase of at least 200% over the previous average. Also monitor the company’s press releases for any mention of revenue growth or new contracts.
expect_next.timing_regime: CWR appears to be in the very early stage of a potential recovery. The lack of a clear crash bottom or a defined support level means we are still waiting for the first signs of capitulation to be fully exhausted.

buy_vs_sell.lean: Neutral – the data does not yet support a clear contrarian bullish case.
buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If the partnership with Delta and Centrica delivers a tangible product pipeline, the company could tap a growing market and see its share price rise toward the mid‑range of its 52‑week band. A 30‑40% upside from the current level would be a meaningful gain for a low‑cap firm.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: Without a clear earnings story or product launch, the company risks staying in limbo. The thin operating history and the fact that only governance changes have been announced leave room for continued volatility and potential further downside.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside is modest, but the downside is still significant because the stock is thinly traded and lacks a solid earnings base. A 1:2 reward‑to‑risk ratio would require a clear catalyst that moves the price 30% higher.

playbook.if_bullish: Wait for a partnership announcement or a quarterly earnings release. Enter at a break of the 20‑day moving average with a stop a few points below that level to protect against a false breakout.
playbook.if_watching: Keep an eye on volume spikes and any mention of new contracts. A 200% jump in volume on a price move above the 20‑day average would be a trigger for a small, cautious position.
playbook.if_passing: None identified – the company is not facing imminent delisting or bankruptcy, so we do not recommend abandoning the position outright.

ai_insights.bottom_line: CWR Ltd. is still in a pre‑catalyst phase; only a clear partnership announcement or earnings release can justify a contrarian bet, and the upside remains modest while the downside is still substantial.
